name: Abydos Technology
description: Abydos Technology refers to a pseudoscientific theory that certain hieroglyphs found in the Temple of Seti I at Abydos, Egypt, depict advanced technological devices, specifically resembling helicopters, submarines, and airplanes. Proponents of this theory suggest that these hieroglyphs are evidence of either time travel, extraterrestrial contact, or a highly advanced, lost civilization predating dynastic Egypt. The theory gained traction through internet forums, documentaries, and books promoting alternative historical interpretations. The 'helicopter hieroglyph' is the most famous example. Mainstream Egyptologists dismiss this interpretation. They explain the perceived resemblance as a result of palimpsests, where layers of different hieroglyphs are superimposed over time. The original hieroglyphs were carved during the reign of Seti I, but later, during the reign of Ramesses II, the temple was modified, and some hieroglyphs were plastered over and re-carved. Over time, the plaster eroded, revealing traces of the older hieroglyphs beneath, creating the illusion of modern technology. Furthermore, Egyptologists argue that the hieroglyphs, when correctly interpreted within the context of ancient Egyptian symbolism and language, have standard religious and royal meanings, relating to the pharaoh's power and connection to the gods. There is no credible archaeological or linguistic evidence to support the claims of advanced technology or extraterrestrial influence.
subtitle: Ancient Hieroglyphs or Advanced Technology?

claims.evidence: Resemblance to helicopters, submarines, and airplanes in the Abydos hieroglyphs.
claims.criticism: Result of palimpsests (superimposed hieroglyphs) and pareidolia (seeing patterns in random stimuli).

claims.claim: Ancient Egyptians possessed advanced technology or knowledge from extraterrestrial beings.
claims.evidence: The existence of the 'helicopter hieroglyphs' and other anomalies that supposedly cannot be explained by conventional understanding of ancient Egyptian technology.
claims.criticism: Lack of supporting archaeological evidence. Conventional Egyptology offers plausible explanations for perceived anomalies.

claims.claim: The hieroglyphs are proof of time travel or a lost, highly advanced civilization.
claims.evidence: The presence of depictions resembling future technology suggests either time travelers visited ancient Egypt or a highly advanced civilization existed before dynastic Egypt.
claims.criticism: Occam's Razor: The simplest explanation (palimpsests and misinterpretations) is more likely than extraordinary claims requiring extraordinary evidence, which is lacking.

keyFigures.name: Erich von Däniken
keyFigures.role: Popularized the idea of ancient astronauts and extraterrestrial influence on ancient civilizations.

keyFigures.name: David Childress
keyFigures.role: Promotes alternative history theories, including the Abydos Technology theory, in his books and documentaries.

keyFigures.name: Frank Yurco
keyFigures.role: Egyptologist who has debunked the Abydos helicopter theory and explained it as a palimpsest.

mythologyConnections: Osiris myth (Abydos as a cult center)
Royal power and divine connection of the pharaohs

timeline.event: Reign of Seti I: Temple of Seti I constructed at Abydos.
Reign of Ramesses II: Temple modified, hieroglyphs altered.
The 'helicopter hieroglyphs' begin to circulate in alternative history circles.
The theory gains widespread popularity through the internet and documentaries.

sources.author: David Childress
sources.work: Technology of the Gods: The Incredible Sciences of the Ancients
sources.title: David Childress

sources.author: K. Lal Gauri
sources.work: The Deterioration of Egyptian Limestone Monuments
sources.title: K. Lal Gauri
